perturbation.original_text: If the recipient is forced to expose the confide ntial information due to a legal regulation, a lega lly valid judgement or a non-disputable official decision or decree in part or in whole, it shall immediately inform the o wner of the confidential information in writing so that the own er can take measures to protect the confidential in formation.
perturbation.changed_text: If the recipient is forced to expose the confidential information due to a legal regulation, a legally valid judgement or a non-disputable official decision or decree in part or in whole.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the obligation to inform the owner of confidential information when disclosure is legally compelled creates a legal risk. German law requires notification and consultation in such situations to allow the owner to explore protective measures.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Civil Code § 241(2) establishes a duty of care and consideration in contractual relationships. This includes a duty to inform the other party if actions (like legally compelled disclosure) might affect their interests. Omitting the notification requirement violates this duty.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 241 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 241 Pflichten aus dem Schuldverhältnis (1) Kraft des Schuldverhältnisses ist der Gläubiger berechtigt, von dem Schuldner eine Leistung zu fordern. Die Leistung kann auch in einem Unterlassen bestehen. (2) Das Schuldverhältnis kann nach seinem Inhalt jeden Teil zur Rücksicht auf die Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen des anderen Teils verpflichten. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The omission of the notification requirement directly contradicts the duty of care and consideration established by German law, as explained by the LLM. The original text ensures that the owner can take protective measures. Removing this creates a legal loophole, as the recipient no longer needs to inform the owner, potentially harming the owner's interests. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing § 241 BGB, states that the Schuldverhältnis (contractual relationship) can obligate each party to Rücksicht auf die Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen des anderen Teils (consider the rights, legal goods, and interests of the other party). Omitting the notification requirement fails to meet this standard of consideration.

perturbation.original_text: At the end of the contract the receiving party s hall return the confidential information and any po ssibly extant copies after receipt of a written request, provided that this is technically feasible and does not vio late the retention obligations. As an alternative to that, the receivi ng party can also ensure in writing the destruction of the received documentation if that is technically feasible.
perturbation.changed_text: At the end of the contract the receiving party shall return the confidential information and any possibly extant copies after receipt of a written request. As an alternative to that, the receiving party can also ensure in writing the destruction of the received documentation.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'provided that this is technically feasible and does not violate the retention obligations' eliminates a crucial condition that acknowledges legal retention requirements under German law. This omission could lead to the unlawful destruction of documents that must be retained for legal or regulatory purposes.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Commercial Code § 257 mandates specific retention periods for business records. By removing the clause that acknowledges retention obligations, the agreement could be interpreted as requiring the destruction of documents that must be legally retained, thus violating this provision.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 257 HGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Handelsgesetzbuch § 257 Aufbewahrung von Unterlagen Aufbewahrungsfristen (1) Jeder Kaufmann ist verpflichtet, die folgenden Unterlagen geordnet aufzubewahren: 1. Handelsbücher, Inventare, Eröffnungsbilanzen, Jahresabschlüsse, Einzelabschlüsse nach § 325 Abs. 2a, Lageberichte, Konzernabschlüsse, Konzernlageberichte sowie die zu ihrem Verständnis erforderlichen Arbeitsanweisungen und sonstigen Organisationsunterlagen, 2. die empfangenen Handelsbriefe, 3. Wiedergaben der abgesandten Handelsbriefe, 4. Belege für Buchungen in den von ihm nach § 238 Abs. 1 zu führenden Büchern (Buchungsbelege). (2) Handelsbriefe sind nur Schriftstücke, die ein Handelsgeschäft betreffen. (3) Mit Ausnahme der Eröffnungsbilanzen und Abschlüsse können die in Absatz 1 aufgeführten Unterlagen auch als Wiedergabe auf einem Bildträger oder auf anderen Datenträgern aufbewahrt werden, wenn dies den Grundsätzen ordnungsmäßiger Buchführung entspricht und sichergestellt ist, daß die Wiedergabe oder die Daten 1. mit den empfangenen Handelsbriefen und den Buchungsbelegen bildlich und mit den anderen Unterlagen inhaltlich übereinstimmen, wenn sie lesbar gemacht werden, 2. während der Dauer der Aufbewahrungsfrist verfügbar sind und jederzeit innerhalb angemessener Frist lesbar gemacht werden können. Sind Unterlagen auf Grund des § 239 Abs. 4 Satz 1 auf Datenträgern hergestellt worden, können statt des Datenträgers die Daten auch ausgedruckt aufbewahrt werden; die ausgedruckten Unterlagen können auch nach Satz 1 aufbewahrt werden. (4) Die in Absatz 1 Nummer 1 aufgeführten Unterlagen sind zehn Jahre, die in Absatz 1 Nummer 4 aufgeführten Unterlagen acht Jahre und die sonstigen in Absatz 1 aufgeführten Unterlagen sechs Jahre aufzubewahren. (5) Die Aufbewahrungsfrist beginnt mit dem Schluß des Kalenderjahrs, in dem die letzte Eintragung in das Handelsbuch gemacht, das Inventar aufgestellt, die Eröffnungsbilanz oder der Jahresabschluß festgestel

perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase 'provided that this is technically feasible and does not violate the retention obligations.' This omission directly contradicts German Commercial Code § 257, as stated in both scraped law snippets, which mandates specific retention periods for business records ('Jeder Kaufmann ist verpflichtet, die folgenden Unterlagen geordnet aufzubewahren'). By removing the clause that acknowledges retention obligations, the agreement could be interpreted as requiring the destruction of documents that must be legally retained, thus violating this provision.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that for this agreement Germa n law applies excluding the United Nations Conventi on on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The legal venue for all disputes arising from this agre ement is the head office of the company Helukabel in Hemmingen.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that for this agreement, the legal venue for all disputes arising from this agreement is the head office of the company Helukabel in Hemmingen.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying that German law applies, the agreement creates uncertainty about the governing law. This can contradict mandatory provisions of German law that would otherwise apply to the agreement, such as those related to contractual obligations and consumer protection. Without specifying which law governs, some protections could be unintentionally avoided.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Civil Code (BGB) - General Principles of Contract Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The German Civil Code (BGB) provides fundamental principles governing contracts. Omitting the clause specifying German law could create ambiguity that circumvents these principles. German law has provisions regarding good faith and fair dealing, that provide consumer protection and would govern the interpretation of this agreement. Removing the explicit mention of the law, would not explicitly contradict but creates a risk by allowing the potential interpretation of other laws.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that German law applies, excluding the United Nations Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The changed text omits this clause, creating uncertainty about the governing law. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation suggest that this omission can contradict mandatory provisions of German law, specifically regarding consumer protection and contractual obligations. The BGB snippet confirms that German law encompasses various aspects of legal capacity, associations, and obligations, and creates a strong argument that omitting the governing law clause undermines the application of these protections, as without it, the contract could potentially be interpreted under different, less protective legal frameworks.

perturbation.original_text: The confidential information will be provided a s is. The disclosing party does not assume any warr anty for the correctness, completeness or usability of the confi dential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidential information will be provided as is.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the statement that 'The disclosing party does not assume any warranty for the correctness, completeness or usability of the confidential information' could unintentionally imply a warranty where none is intended. Under German law, the lack of an explicit disclaimer might lead to claims that the disclosing party implicitly warranted the suitability of the information for a specific purpose.
perturbation.law_explanation: German Civil Code § 444 addresses warranty exclusion in sales contracts. While this agreement isn't a sales contract, removing the disclaimer about warranty could, by analogy, create an implied warranty claim under German law, especially if the information is used for a commercial purpose.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 444 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 444 Haftungsausschluss Auf eine Vereinbarung, durch welche die Rechte des Käufers wegen eines Mangels ausgeschlossen oder beschränkt werden, kann sich der Verkäufer nicht berufen, soweit er den Mangel arglistig verschwiegen oder eine Garantie für die Beschaffenheit der Sache übernommen hat. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The omission of the disclaimer regarding warranty creates a legal contradiction. The original text explicitly states that 'The disclosing party does not assume any warranty for the correctness, completeness or usability of the confi dential information.' By removing this, the changed text opens the door for an implied warranty claim, especially if the information is used for commercial purposes. This contradicts the intention to exclude warranties, potentially violating German law. The scraped snippets of § 444 BGB, specifically the phrase 'Haftungsausschluss' (exclusion of liability) and the section discussing the exclusion or restriction of the buyer's rights due to a defect, implies that explicit disclaimers are necessary to prevent warranty claims. The omission of this disclaimer directly undermines the intent to exclude any warranty, therefore creating a legal contradiction.
